subject: Cloud Computing
title: Cloud Revenue: A Trillion-Dollar Market
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Cloud Revenue: A Trillion-Dollar Market.

hook.text: AWS generates over $90 billion in annual revenue. Azure and Google add another $90 billion combined.

buildup.text: Global cloud spending exceeds $500 billion per year and grows 20% annually. It's one of tech's biggest markets.

discovery.text: Cloud revenue is recurring: monthly subscriptions that rarely cancel. Investors love the predictability.

twist.text: AWS alone is more profitable than Amazon's entire retail business. Cloud subsidizes the everything store.

climax.text: Cloud is no longer a tech niche. It's a fundamental utility — like electricity or telecommunications.

punchline.text: Cloud isn't a product category. It's an industry now.

quiz.question: Why do investors favor cloud company revenues?
options: Cloud revenue is recurring and predictable with low cancellation rates
Cloud companies never have to pay for physical infrastructure
Government subsidies guarantee cloud company profits
